prompt: Major refactor of flow to a modular "plugin" design — turn flow from a personal, hard-wired toolchain into a professional, modular product: each user installs only what their stack needs (conditional stack/integration modules: svelte, tailwind, supabase, cloudflare, copilot, agy/research), tmux becomes an optional launcher backend (everything can run in a plain claude shell), context usage is aggressively optimized, remaining recurring fan-outs become named Claude Code custom sub-agents with pinned model routing, and the end-state is Claude Code plugin/marketplace packaging with per-repo enablement.

features.title: Target-architecture doc (ideal, gap analysis, module map, roadmap)
features.description: Author docs/plugin-architecture.md, the single source of truth for flow's modular redesign: `## Ideal flow` (design-blind target: plugin distribution, file-based runtime state with tmux as one launcher backend among several, three-tier progressive disclosure, named custom agents with pinned model routing, problem-first planning artifacts), `## Gap analysis` (ideal vs current flow with file evidence, e.g. liveness = windowExists in bin/lib/feature.ts and bin/lib/done.ts), `## Module map` (every skill under skills/*/, every agents/*.md, every PATH-bound helper and validator assigned to exactly one module; v1 module set: core (mandatory), stack-svelte (incl. the testing skill), stack-tailwind-shadcn, stack-supabase, stack-cloudflare-pages, copilot, research (agy delegation + research helpers + flow-plan-review)), and `## Roadmap` (phases 1-6 with entry/exit criteria, incl. the crash-safe liveness requirement (PID + process-start-time, per AGY pre-mortem) for Phase 3 and the fan-out-to-agent consolidation map with model pins for Phase 4). PRIOR ART — reuse, do not re-derive: the ratified feature-grain PRD for Phase 1 (.flow-tmp/plan.md in the epic-design worktree, summarized durably in .flow/epics/major-refactor-flow-modular-plugin/design.md) already contains the ideal-flow prose, the gap-analysis table, the v1 module set, and the AGY-reviewed decision analysis (D1 module-layer-now-plugins-later, D2 global-selectable-now, D3 tmux-optional-default-on, D4 measure-then-tighten); this PR lands that content with elaboration, it does not re-plan.
features.rationale: Hides D1 (the target-architecture content — the most review-tuned part); the seam artifact every phase-opening node consumes; the walking-skeleton root.
acceptanceCriteria: WHEN docs/plugin-architecture.md is merged THE SYSTEM SHALL contain the four sections ## Ideal flow, ## Gap analysis, ## Module map, ## Roadmap (grep-checkable) and pass flow-md-validate
WHEN the module map is inspected THE SYSTEM SHALL list every shipped skill, agent, PATH-bound helper, and validator exactly once (no orphans, no double assignment)

features.title: Module registry + selection-aware flow install
features.description: Add bin/lib/modules.ts — a typed, pure-data module registry (module id -> skills[], agents[], helpers[], validators[]; core mandatory; no behaviour, so a Phase-6 plugin re-cut edits rows not logic) encoding the ## Module map from docs/plugin-architecture.md, with a completeness lint in bin/lib/modules.test.ts proving every dir under skills/*/, every agents/*.md, and every PATH-bound helper maps to exactly one module — and make flow install selection-aware: --modules <csv> / --all / --core-only flags with mutual-exclusion and unknown-id validation (exit non-zero naming the id, link nothing), a TTY-gated first-run Q&A via the existing confirm.ts injection seam, selection persisted at ~/.flow/config.json `modules` (tolerant boundary reader mirroring models-config.ts), --upgrade preserving the recorded selection without re-asking, non-TTY defaulting to core with a one-line notice naming --modules/--all, and pruning of deselected modules' symlinks via the existing manifest machinery. --all must remain byte-identical to today's unconditional install (set-equality spec). PRIOR ART: this node corresponds to Tasks 2-6 of the ratified Phase-1 feature-grain PRD referenced in .flow/epics/major-refactor-flow-modular-plugin/design.md — reuse its task breakdown and acceptance criteria rather than re-planning.
features.rationale: Hides D2 (distribution mechanism: module layer as pure data now, plugin boundaries later). Consumes p1-design-doc's ## Module map as the registry's source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: WHEN flow install --modules core,research runs on a fresh target THE SYSTEM SHALL link only those modules' artifacts, and a later narrower selection SHALL prune previously-linked others (vitest, injectable targets)
WHEN flow install --all runs THE SYSTEM SHALL produce a symlink set byte-identical to today's unconditional install (set-equality spec)
WHEN an unknown module id is passed THE SYSTEM SHALL exit non-zero naming it and link nothing
WHEN a selection is recorded THE SYSTEM SHALL have flow install --upgrade re-link the same set without prompting

features.title: Graceful degradation for deselected modules
features.description: Make module deselection safe end-to-end: when a module is not in the recorded selection (read via the bin/lib/modules.ts registry + ~/.flow/config.json modules key), every pipeline path that depends on it degrades gracefully with a named notice instead of failing or silently misbehaving — copilot deselected: flow-request-copilot / copilot-classify steps skip with a notice; research deselected: the forced-research Step-1.5 path, flow-research-cache/run/note, and flow-plan-review skip cleanly (mirroring the existing graceful-skip-sans-agy pattern); stack modules deselected: their skills are simply absent from the routing table. Add a doctor-style summary to flow install output naming which optional modules are inactive and why. Tests assert each skip path emits its named notice and exits 0, and that a full selection changes nothing.
features.rationale: Hides D4 (module-absence behaviour) behind the selection contract; Phase-6 per-repo absence reuses this contract unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: WHEN a pipeline step needs a deselected module THE SYSTEM SHALL skip with a named notice and exit 0 (a test per skip path)
WHEN flow install completes THE SYSTEM SHALL print a doctor-style summary naming inactive optional modules and why

features.title: Per-repo activation evaluation (global install, repo-local activation)
features.description: Evaluate the AGY-surfaced middle branch 'global install, per-repo activation': keep one global install but let a repo activate a subset of modules (repo-local skill linking or an activation file consumed at link/session time), so multi-stack machines stop paying the skill-frontmatter routing-table context tax in every repo before plugin packaging arrives. Deliverable: a Context/Decision/Consequences addendum to docs/plugin-architecture.md recording the verdict with evidence (incl. the measured frontmatter token cost of installed-but-irrelevant skills, if the Phase-5 audit has not yet produced it, via a quick measurement); if affirmative, ship the minimal activation mechanism with tests; if negative, record exactly what Phase-6 plugin packaging must deliver instead. Consumes the bin/lib/modules.ts registry as the unit of activation. This node is spike-shaped and may legitimately resolve docs-only.
features.rationale: Hides D3's open branch (per-repo granularity before plugins) behind a recorded decision + optional mechanism.
acceptanceCriteria: WHEN the evaluation completes THE SYSTEM SHALL have a Context/Decision/Consequences addendum in docs/plugin-architecture.md recording the per-repo-activation verdict with evidence
WHEN the verdict is affirmative THE SYSTEM SHALL include the minimal activation mechanism with tests; WHEN negative THE SYSTEM SHALL record the requirement Phase 6 must satisfy instead

features.title: Crash-safe file-based pipeline liveness signal
features.description: Add a crash-safe, stale-tolerant file-based liveness signal for pipelines, per the Phase-3 spec in docs/plugin-architecture.md's roadmap (AGY D3 pre-mortem: bare heartbeats go stale): record PID + process-start-time in ~/.flow/state/<slug>.json at launch, and add a small helper returning alive/dead/stale verdicts (PID exists AND start-time matches = alive; PID recycled = dead). Wire flow ls, flow done's completion checks, and flow feature create's collision detection to consult the file signal, retaining windowExists (bin/lib/feature.ts, bin/lib/done.ts) as a secondary signal for tmux-launched pipelines during the transition. Old state files without the new fields must degrade to today's behaviour, not crash. Tests cover: live process, dead PID, recycled PID (start-time mismatch), and missing fields.
features.rationale: Hides D5 (the liveness representation) behind an alive/dead/stale helper; the precondition for any non-tmux launcher.
acceptanceCriteria: WHEN a pipeline is launched THE SYSTEM SHALL record PID + process-start-time in ~/.flow/state/<slug>.json
WHEN the liveness helper checks a dead or recycled PID THE SYSTEM SHALL report not-alive (tests cover live, dead, recycled-PID, and missing-field cases)

features.title: Launcher backend abstraction + --no-tmux
features.description: Introduce a launcher-backend abstraction so tmux becomes optional (ratified decision D3/D6: tmux stays the default backend, plain mode is opt-in): a backend interface (launch, attach-hint; liveness already handled by the shared file signal from p3-file-liveness) with two implementations — tmux (default, current behaviour unchanged) and plain-terminal (runs the pipeline's claude session without tmux) — selected by flow feature create --no-tmux, a config default, or tmux absence, with precedence flag > config > autodetect. flow ls / flow done / collision checks must behave identically under both backends via the file-based liveness signal. Tests cover backend selection precedence and the no-tmux launch path; the default path stays byte-compatible with today's tmux behaviour.
features.rationale: Hides D6 (launch mechanics) behind a backend interface; tmux demotes to one implementation. Consumes p3-file-liveness's helper API.
acceptanceCriteria: WHEN flow feature create --no-tmux runs THE SYSTEM SHALL launch the pipeline without tmux and flow ls/done SHALL report it via the file-based signal
WHEN no backend is specified and tmux is present THE SYSTEM SHALL default to tmux with existing behaviour unchanged

features.title: tmux-optional docs + plain-mode UX polish
features.description: Document and polish the tmux-optional runtime shipped by p3-launcher-backend: the README install/quickstart no longer lists tmux as a prerequisite (moves it to 'recommended, default backend'), a short doc section covers running pipelines in a plain shell (launch with --no-tmux, monitor via flow ls, the resume story) and when tmux is worth it (parallel pipelines, walk-away/attach), and the plain-mode notice copy surfaced by the launcher backend gets a UX pass. Docs-scope checks (flow-md-validate, prettier) pass; any CLI copy changes are covered by existing-style tests.
features.rationale: Completes D6's user-facing half (onboarding story) once the backend surface it documents has shipped.
acceptanceCriteria: WHEN the README install section is read THE SYSTEM SHALL present tmux as the recommended default backend, not a prerequisite
WHEN docs-scope checks run THE SYSTEM SHALL pass flow-md-validate and lint

features.title: Named custom agents for /pr-review fan-outs
features.description: Promote /pr-review's recurring fan-outs from inline spawn prompts to named custom-agent definitions under agents/ (discovered by discoverAgents, symlinked by flow install, module-assigned in the bin/lib/modules.ts registry), per the Phase-4 consolidation map in docs/plugin-architecture.md's roadmap: the six review lenses, the Step-1.5 gatekeeper (its documented model: haiku cost-routing override moves into frontmatter), and the Step-3.5 consolidator-validator (default sonnet), following the existing agents/flow-verify.md / agents/flow-fix-applier.md pattern (frontmatter model/effort/tools pins; per-spawn model: still wins). Update pr-review's SKILL.md/references to spawn by agent name, keep every artifact contract unchanged (agent-output-<lens>.json, gatekeeper-result.json, consolidator-result.json), and update the bidirectional exemption docs. The nine-Task-exemption set is renamed in place where needed, never widened.
features.rationale: Hides D7 for the /pr-review surface: routing pins move from prose to auditable agent frontmatter, artifact contracts stable.
acceptanceCriteria: WHEN /pr-review fans out THE SYSTEM SHALL spawn the six lenses, gatekeeper, and consolidator as named agents/*.md definitions with their documented model pins (gatekeeper: haiku)
WHEN the artifact contracts are inspected THE SYSTEM SHALL show unchanged output paths and shapes

features.title: Named custom agents for pipeline-side fan-outs
features.description: Promote the remaining pipeline-side recurring fan-outs to named agents/*.md custom-agent definitions with pinned model/effort/tools routing, mirroring p4-review-agents' pattern on its skill surfaces, per the Phase-4 consolidation map in docs/plugin-architecture.md's roadmap: /new-feature's scout, /product-planning's discovery subagent (feature + epic modes), and /flow-pipeline step 10's merge-conflict resolver; evaluate (do not blindly convert) the /coder edit-applier and /epic-run judgment subagent for the same treatment. Keep every documented contract unchanged (artifact paths like .flow-tmp/scout.md and .flow-tmp/merge-resolver-result.json, model overrides), update each spawn site plus the bidirectional exemption docs (AGENTS.md, exemption-contracts.md, each skill's SKILL.md). The nine-Task-exemption set is renamed in place, never widened.
features.rationale: Hides D7 for the pipeline-side surfaces; keeps the exemption contracts stable while making routing declarative.
acceptanceCriteria: WHEN scout, discovery, or the merge-conflict resolver fires THE SYSTEM SHALL spawn a named agents/*.md definition with artifact paths and contracts unchanged
WHEN AGENTS.md and exemption-contracts.md are read THE SYSTEM SHALL document the named agents bidirectionally with the nine-exemption set unwidened

features.title: Per-phase context/token audit + measurement tooling
features.description: Measure before tightening (ratified decision D4 of the prior PRD, per the Phase-5 plan in docs/plugin-architecture.md's roadmap): build a small transcript-analysis helper (reads Claude Code session JSONL under ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/) that attributes context/token spend per pipeline phase and per tool-call class (diffs, verify/failure logs, skill bodies, sub-agent returns, in-process edits), run it on at least one real completed pipeline, and land the method + findings as docs/context-economy-audit.md — including the in-process edit-size distribution needed to judge the /coder routing threshold (items 4.2/7 of the original prompt) and the measured frontmatter cost of installed-but-irrelevant skills. Deliverable is the helper (tested on a committed fixture transcript) plus the baseline report; no pipeline behaviour changes in this PR.
features.rationale: Hides D8's measurement half (where context is actually spent); makes the diet falsifiable instead of dogmatic.
acceptanceCriteria: WHEN the audit helper runs on the fixture transcript THE SYSTEM SHALL attribute token spend per phase and per tool-call class (tested)
WHEN the audit report lands THE SYSTEM SHALL include a real-pipeline baseline and the in-process edit-size distribution

features.title: Context diet: AGENTS.md + skill splits + edit-threshold tuning
features.description: Act on the audit data in docs/context-economy-audit.md (from p5-token-audit): execute the highest-value context reductions it identifies — diet flow's AGENTS.md (519 lines at epic-design time) toward the <200-line research guidance by moving procedural detail into lazily-loaded references, continue the lean-body + lazy-reference splits for the largest SKILL.md bodies (the supervisor split at PR #411 is the pattern), and tighten the in-process edit-routing threshold toward /coder — or adopt the mechanical edit-size guard (hook-enforced cap forcing delegation, the AGY D4 alternative branch) — if and only if the data shows in-process edits are a material spend. Re-run the audit helper after the diet and record the before/after delta in the report. All structural lints (bin/skill-md-lint.test.ts anchors, flow-md-validate) must stay green; renamed anchors update their lints in the same commit.
features.rationale: Hides D8's action half (which cuts are worth making); consumes the audit report that ranks them.
acceptanceCriteria: WHEN the diet lands THE SYSTEM SHALL keep every structural lint green and record a re-measured before/after token delta in the audit report
WHEN AGENTS.md is measured THE SYSTEM SHALL be materially smaller with moved content reachable via lazy references

features.title: Plugin packaging pilot: one stack module end-to-end
features.description: Package exactly ONE stack module (recommend stack-svelte) as a Claude Code plugin end-to-end to prove the distribution model before any mass migration: a .claude-plugin/plugin.json manifest bundling the module's skills (namespaced /flow-svelte:<skill>), a marketplace.json git-repo registry, per-repo enablement, and documented coexistence rules with the symlink install — a repo with the plugin enabled must not double-load the globally-linked copy of the same skill; define the precedence and encode it as an install-time rule or a documented constraint. Consumes the bin/lib/modules.ts registry rows as the module boundary being packaged and the p2-conditional-degradation contract (a module absent in a repo degrades exactly like a deselected one). Lands a written packaging playbook (steps, gotchas, namespacing decisions) that p6-plugin-rollout follows mechanically.
features.rationale: Hides D9's pilot half (the packaging mechanics), discovering ecosystem drift on one module instead of ten; produces the rollout playbook.
acceptanceCriteria: WHEN the pilot plugin is enabled in a repo THE SYSTEM SHALL expose the module's skills namespaced without double-loading globally-linked copies
WHEN the pilot lands THE SYSTEM SHALL include a written packaging playbook for the rollout

features.title: Plugin rollout: all modules, marketplace, helper strategy
features.description: Migrate the remaining modules to Claude Code plugins using p6-plugin-pilot's playbook: the remaining stack + integration modules, then flow-core (pipeline/universal skills plus the named agents/*.md definitions from p4-review-agents and p4-pipeline-agents bundled as plugin agents); decide and document the helper-binary strategy (PATH symlinks stay the always-global CLI's job vs ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}-relative invocation — the ~40 bare-name helper invocations across skills are the binding constraint); marketplace docs + versioning; and the flow install switchover/coexistence story (module selection maps to plugin enablement; this is the first sanctioned break with the --all byte-parity guarantee, stated explicitly in the PR). Ends with per-repo enablement as the primary distribution path and the symlink install documented as the fallback. Expect this node to be re-decomposed against the playbook before execution (recorded in the epic design's Open Questions).
features.rationale: Closes D9 (full migration + switchover) — the diamond-closing integration; consumes the playbook and the named agent files flow-core bundles.
acceptanceCriteria: WHEN the rollout completes THE SYSTEM SHALL distribute every module as a plugin with per-repo enablement primary and the helper-binary strategy decided and documented
WHEN a consumer follows the marketplace docs THE SYSTEM SHALL install flow-core plus chosen modules via the plugin marketplace
